page: Amazon Web Services mac1.metal is a bare metal instance belonging to the mac1 server family, designed to run macOS workloads. It features a dedicated Intel processor with an x86_64_mac architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 6 physical cores and 12 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) with 2 threads per core, supported by 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.67 GB of RAM per physical core.

This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware. Storage must be provisioned externally via network-attached services. For connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The lack of local storage and dedicated GPU accelerators represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring high-speed local disk I/O or hardware-accelerated graphics processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the mac1.metal instance provides dedicated access to physical Apple Mac mini hardware within the AWS cloud infrastructure. This dedicated resource allocation is tailored for specific development pipelines rather than general-purpose multi-tenant computing. The primary workload fit for this instance includes macOS and iOS application building, testing, signing, and continuous integration (CI/CD) pipelines that require native Intel-based macOS environments.
description: Amazon Web Services mac1.metal is a bare metal instance designed for native macOS workloads. Powered by a dedicated 3.2 GHz Intel processor with an x86_64_mac architecture, it features 6 physical cores, 12 vCPUs, and 32.0 GB of system memory. The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps to support data transfer. Notably, mac1.metal does not include local storage or a dedicated GPU, requiring external storage provisioning and limiting its use for graphics-heavy tasks. This hardware profile is qualitatively structured for dedicated resource access, offering a specialized environment for Apple platform development. It is primarily suited for macOS and iOS application compilation, continuous integration, and automated testing pipelines requiring an Intel-based macOS environment.

tagline: Dedicated bare metal Intel-based macOS instances configured for Apple platform application building, testing, and continuous integration pipelines.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel processor with 6 physical cores and 12 vCPUs at 3.2 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing 2.67 GB per core
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external storage and data transfer
No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included
Optimized for macOS and iOS build, test, and CI/CD pipelines
